label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this question. The question is asking about the purpose of using a lot of figures in the text. The options are A to D. First, I need to understand what the text is about. The text talks about credit card usage in China, how it's grown over the years, and some research findings by Rui Yao from the University of Missouri. There are several numbers mentioned: 30% of households own a credit card, 40% growth rate, 90% unaware of safety issues, etc. Now, the question is why the author included so many figures. Let me go through each option. Option A is "record the valuable data." Well, the figures are indeed data points, but the question is about the purpose. Recording data is more like keeping a record, but the text is using the figures to support points made by the researcher. So maybe not the main purpose here. Option B is "provide concrete evidence." That makes sense because the figures are used to back up the claims made by Rui Yao. For example, when she says there's a large growth in credit card usage, the 40% annual growth rate is concrete evidence of that. The other stats about user awareness and payment habits also support her points about the implications of credit card use. So this seems like a possible answer. Option C is "introduce a research method." The text doesn't really explain how the research was conducted. It just presents the findings with numbers. So probably not C. Option D is "give a detailed description." While the numbers do describe the situation, the main purpose isn't just to describe in detail but to provide evidence for the researcher's arguments. But maybe detailed description is part of it. However, I think B is
